name: Yue Fei
description: Yue Fei, posthumously known as the Prince of E, is a revered historical figure in Chinese culture, often elevated to the status of a deity or cultural hero. Born during the Song Dynasty, Yue Fei distinguished himself as a brilliant military strategist and a paragon of loyalty and patriotism. His unwavering dedication to the Song Empire and his exceptional leadership in repelling the Jurchen invaders solidified his place in Chinese history. Despite his military successes and immense popularity, Yue Fei was tragically framed for treason and executed, a decision widely condemned as a miscarriage of justice. This unjust death further cemented his status as a martyr and a symbol of integrity. Over time, Yue Fei has been deified, with temples and shrines dedicated to his memory throughout China. He is venerated as a protector, a symbol of martial prowess, and an embodiment of unwavering loyalty, righteousness, and filial piety. His story continues to inspire generations, serving as a potent reminder of the importance of unwavering principles and selfless service to one's nation.
subtitle: The Loyal General
symbols: Calligraphy of 'Serve the Country with Utmost Loyalty'

keyMyths.title: Yue Fei's Tattoo
keyMyths.description: According to legend, Yue Fei's mother tattooed the words "Serve the Country with Utmost Loyalty" (精忠報國) on his back to remind him of his duty to the Song Dynasty. This act symbolizes the profound influence of familial duty and the unwavering commitment to national service. The tattoo served as a constant reminder of his purpose, guiding his actions and solidifying his reputation as a paragon of patriotism. This image is frequently depicted in art and literature, reinforcing the idea of Yue Fei as a symbol of unwavering loyalty.
keyMyths.source: Traditional Chinese folklore and historical accounts

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Yancheng
keyMyths.description: The Battle of Yancheng is one of Yue Fei's most celebrated military victories. Leading a smaller force against a much larger Jurchen army, Yue Fei employed innovative tactics and inspired his troops to fight with unmatched courage. His strategic brilliance and the unwavering loyalty of his soldiers resulted in a decisive victory that significantly weakened the Jurchen advance. This battle became a testament to Yue Fei's military genius and his ability to inspire his troops, solidifying his reputation as an exceptional military leader.
keyMyths.source: History of Song, official dynastic history

keyMyths.title: Yue Fei's Unjust Execution
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei's tragic downfall stemmed from political machinations and the treachery of Qin Hui, a corrupt official who feared Yue Fei's growing power. Qin Hui fabricated charges of treason against Yue Fei, leading to his unjust imprisonment and execution. This act of injustice sparked widespread outrage and solidified Yue Fei's status as a martyr. The circumstances surrounding his death are considered a profound betrayal and a symbol of the dangers of political corruption and the importance of standing up for what is right, even in the face of adversity.

sources.author: James T.C. Liu

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Yue Fei's unwavering devotion to the Song Dynasty is a core aspect of his legend.

associations.name: Patriotism
associations.significance: He is seen as the embodiment of love for one's country and willingness to defend it.

associations.name: The Tattoo on His Back
associations.significance: His mother's tattoo, 'Serve the country with utmost loyalty,' is a powerful symbol of filial piety and national duty.

associations.name: Spear
associations.significance: Represents his martial prowess and military leadership.

associations.name: Filial Piety
associations.significance: His respect and obedience to his mother are integral to his heroic image.

associations.name: Crane
associations.significance: Sometimes associated with longevity and righteousness, reflecting his enduring legacy.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both were citizen-soldiers who left their farms to lead armies and defend their nations.
Both figures inspired nationalistic fervor and led armies against invaders, fueled by a sense of divine purpose and patriotism.
Both are celebrated for their unwavering loyalty and tragic deaths in service to their emperors.

worshipPractices.name: Yue Fei Temple Visits
worshipPractices.description: Temples dedicated to Yue Fei are visited by people seeking blessings for loyalty, courage, and success. Offerings are made, and prayers are recited.

worshipPractices.name: Opera and Storytelling
worshipPractices.description: Performances of Yue Fei's life story, particularly his battles and tragic end, are common forms of honoring him and reinforcing his values.

cultural.modernLegacy: Yue Fei remains a potent symbol of patriotism and integrity in modern China. His story is frequently invoked in political discourse and popular culture to promote national unity and ethical conduct. Statues and memorials dedicated to him can be found throughout China, serving as reminders of his sacrifice and unwavering commitment to his country.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Influences
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei was born in 1103 in Xiangzhou, Henan province, during the Northern Song Dynasty. From a young age, he displayed exceptional strength and a keen interest in military strategy. His family, though of humble origins, instilled in him a strong sense of patriotism and loyalty. According to legend, his mother tattooed the words 'Serve the country with utmost loyalty' on his back to remind him of his duty. This act became a powerful symbol of filial piety and national service. Yue Fei received rigorous training in martial arts and military tactics, quickly mastering various weapons and strategies. He excelled in archery and swordsmanship, demonstrating remarkable skill and determination. His early experiences shaped his character and prepared him for his future role as a military leader, laying the foundation for his unwavering commitment to defending the Song Empire against its enemies.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Military Achievements
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei's military career began during the Jurchen invasion of the Northern Song Dynasty. He quickly rose through the ranks, demonstrating exceptional leadership and tactical brilliance. He led his troops to numerous victories against the Jurchen forces, effectively halting their advance and reclaiming lost territories. His most famous campaigns include the defense of Xiangyang and the recovery of several key cities in Henan. Yue Fei's strategies were innovative and effective, often employing unconventional tactics to outmaneuver his enemies. He was known for his discipline and his ability to inspire his troops. His soldiers were fiercely loyal to him, and his army became one of the most formidable forces in the Song Dynasty. Despite facing numerous challenges, including political intrigue and limited resources, Yue Fei remained steadfast in his determination to defend his country. His military achievements earned him widespread recognition and cemented his reputation as a national hero.

extendedContent.title: Tragic End and Enduring Legacy
extendedContent.content: Despite his military successes, Yue Fei's career was cut short by political machinations. The Song Emperor Zhao Gou, influenced by the treacherous court official Qin Hui, sought a peaceful resolution with the Jurchens, even if it meant sacrificing territory and making concessions. Qin Hui, driven by personal animosity and political ambition, framed Yue Fei for treason. In 1142, Yue Fei was unjustly imprisoned and executed. His death sparked outrage and resentment among the people, who revered him as a national hero. Despite his tragic end, Yue Fei's legacy endured. He became a symbol of patriotism, loyalty, and integrity. His story has been passed down through generations, inspiring countless individuals to serve their country and uphold their principles. Temples and memorials dedicated to Yue Fei can be found throughout China, serving as reminders of his sacrifice and unwavering commitment to his nation. He continues to be celebrated as one of China's greatest military heroes and a paragon of virtue.

shortDescription: Yue Fei was a Song Dynasty general, a symbol of patriotism and loyalty. Falsely accused of treason, his story inspires courage and integrity.
